# CalorieWize

> Calories & Nutrition Facts

Find calories and nutrition facts for thousands of foods. Compare foods side by side. Data from USDA FoodData Central.

## Core pages
- [Methodology and data sources](https://caloriewize.com/methodology/): how every figure is derived, with primary-source citations
- [Food directory](https://caloriewize.com/food/): browse every food we cover
- [Compare](https://caloriewize.com/compare/): side-by-side comparison
- [About and editorial standards](https://caloriewize.com/about/): authorship and review policy

## Key food
- [Low Calorie](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-calorie/)
- [High Protein](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-protein/)
- [High Fiber](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-fiber/)
- [Low Sodium](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-sodium/)
- [Low Sugar](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-sugar/)
- [Low Fat](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-fat/)
- [Low Carb](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-carb/)
- [High Vitamin C](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-vitamin-c/)
- [High Calcium](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-calcium/)
- [High Iron](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-iron/)
- [High Potassium](https://caloriewize.com/list/high-potassium/)
- [Low Cholesterol](https://caloriewize.com/list/low-cholesterol/)

## Data
- Primary sources: USDA FoodData Central, U.S. Food and Drug Administration — Daily Value reference, FAO/WHO Expert Consultation on Protein Quality (DIAAS, Report 92), NIH Office of Dietary Supplements
- All figures are derived from these primary sources; see /methodology/ for derivation and vintage.
